text:
Once upon a time, in a land far away, there lived a clever fox named Felix. Felix was not your ordinary fox; he possessed a remarkable talent for solving puzzles and riddles. His home, nestled near a grand oak tree, served as his workshop, library, and the source of endless entertainment.

Felix had a knack for making friends with all sorts of creatures, but his closest companions were the mischievous weasels and the curious forest animals. Every day, Felix would engage them in his favorite pastime: the brain-teasing riddles.

One morning, after a particularly productive day of riddles, Felix gathered his companions under the shade of the oak tree. "My dear friends," he began, his green eyes twinkling with mischief, "today I have a challenge for you all!"

The weasels huddled closely, ears perked up, while the forest animals snorted with anticipation.
